narratives.description: The creation of the first human, the angels' prostration, Iblis's refusal, and the descent from Paradise

chapters.title: Creation & the Angels' Prostration
chapters.summary: Allah tells the angels He will place a vicegerent on earth. He teaches Adam the names of all things and commands the angels to prostrate — Iblis refuses out of arrogance.

chapters.title: The Forbidden Tree
chapters.summary: Adam and his wife dwell in Paradise but are deceived by Shaytan into eating from the forbidden tree.

chapters.title: Repentance & Descent
chapters.summary: Adam repents and Allah accepts his repentance. Adam and his wife are sent to earth with guidance.

chapters.title: The Two Sons (Habil & Qabil)
chapters.summary: The story of Adam's two sons — one offering is accepted and the other is not, leading to the first murder on earth.

narratives.description: Nuh's 950-year mission of patient preaching, the great flood, and the salvation of believers

chapters.title: The Long Preaching
chapters.summary: Nuh calls his people to worship Allah alone for centuries, but they refuse and put their fingers in their ears.

chapters.title: Building the Ark
chapters.summary: Allah commands Nuh to build the ark. The disbelievers mock him as he constructs it.

chapters.title: The Great Flood
chapters.summary: The oven bursts forth with water. Nuh's son refuses to board and perishes. The ark settles on Mount Judi.

chapters.title: A Father's Grief
chapters.summary: Nuh asks Allah about his son. Allah teaches him that his son was not of his family in faith.

narratives.description: Ibrahim's search for truth, his confrontation with idolatry, the trial of sacrificing his son, and building the Ka'bah

chapters.title: Seeking the Truth
chapters.summary: Ibrahim observes the stars, moon, and sun, rejecting each as they set, until he declares his devotion to the One Creator.

chapters.title: Confronting His Father & People
chapters.summary: Ibrahim smashes the idols and challenges his people. They throw him into a fire, but Allah makes the fire cool and safe.

chapters.title: The Sacrifice
chapters.summary: Ibrahim sees in a dream that he must sacrifice his son. Both submit to Allah's will, and Allah replaces the son with a great sacrifice.

chapters.title: Building the Ka'bah
chapters.summary: Ibrahim and Isma'il raise the foundations of the Ka'bah in Mecca and pray for their descendants.

chapters.title: Ibrahim's Guests
chapters.summary: Angels visit Ibrahim with glad tidings of a son. They reveal they are sent to destroy the people of Lut.

narratives.title_english: The Story of Prophet Musa (Moses) & Pharaoh
narratives.description: The most frequently told story in the Quran — Musa's birth, mission to Pharaoh, the Exodus, and Sinai

chapters.title: Birth & Rescue
chapters.summary: Musa's mother places him in the river to save him from Pharaoh's decree. He is raised in Pharaoh's palace.

chapters.title: Flight to Madyan
chapters.summary: Musa accidentally kills a man, flees to Madyan, helps two women water their flock, and marries one of them.

chapters.title: The Burning Bush
chapters.summary: At the valley of Tuwa, Allah speaks to Musa from the burning bush, grants him signs (the staff and the glowing hand), and commands him to go to Pharaoh.

chapters.title: Confronting Pharaoh
chapters.summary: Musa and Harun confront Pharaoh. The sorcerers are defeated, believe, and face Pharaoh's wrath.

chapters.title: The Exodus & Parting of the Sea
chapters.summary: Allah commands Musa to lead the Israelites by night. The sea parts, they cross safely, and Pharaoh drowns.

chapters.title: Mount Sinai & the Golden Calf
chapters.summary: Musa receives the Torah on Mount Sinai for 40 nights. Meanwhile, his people worship a golden calf made by Samiri.

chapters.title: Musa & Khidr
chapters.summary: Musa journeys with Khidr who performs puzzling acts — damaging a boat, killing a youth, rebuilding a wall — each with divine wisdom.

narratives.description: The best of stories — Yusuf's journey from a dream to a well, from prison to kingship in Egypt

chapters.title: The Dream
chapters.summary: Young Yusuf tells his father Ya'qub about his dream of eleven stars, the sun, and the moon prostrating to him.

chapters.title: The Brothers' Betrayal
chapters.summary: Yusuf's brothers plot against him out of jealousy, throw him into a well, and bring a false blood-stained shirt to their father.

chapters.title: Sold into Slavery
chapters.summary: A caravan finds Yusuf and sells him in Egypt. The minister's household takes him in.

chapters.title: The Minister's Wife
chapters.summary: The minister's wife attempts to seduce Yusuf. He resists, is falsely accused, and is imprisoned.

chapters.title: Prison & Interpreting Dreams
chapters.summary: In prison, Yusuf interprets dreams for two inmates and later for the king — seven fat cows devoured by seven lean ones.

chapters.title: Rise to Power
chapters.summary: The king summons Yusuf, clears his name, and appoints him over the storehouses of Egypt.

chapters.title: Reunion with Brothers
chapters.summary: Yusuf's brothers come to Egypt during the famine, not recognizing him. After tests of character, he reveals himself and forgives them.

chapters.title: Family Reunited
chapters.summary: Ya'qub regains his sight upon receiving Yusuf's shirt. The entire family comes to Egypt, fulfilling the childhood dream.

narratives.title_english: The Stories of Dawud & Sulaiman (David & Solomon)
narratives.description: Father and son prophets blessed with wisdom, kingdom, and power over nature

chapters.title: Dawud Defeats Jalut (Goliath)
chapters.summary: The young Dawud kills Jalut in battle. Allah gives him the kingdom and wisdom.

chapters.title: Dawud's Gifts
chapters.summary: Mountains and birds glorify Allah with Dawud. He is taught to make armor from iron.

chapters.title: Dawud's Test
chapters.summary: Two disputants come to Dawud. He realizes it is a test from Allah, falls in prostration, and repents.

chapters.title: Sulaiman's Kingdom
chapters.summary: Sulaiman commands the wind, jinn, and understands the speech of birds and ants. His vast kingdom serves Allah's purpose.

chapters.title: Sulaiman & the Queen of Sheba
chapters.summary: A hoopoe brings news of Sheba. Sulaiman invites the queen to Islam. Her throne is brought in the blink of an eye. She submits to Allah.

narratives.title_english: The Story of Isa & Maryam (Jesus & Mary)
narratives.description: The miraculous birth of Isa to the virgin Maryam, his mission, and his nature as a prophet

chapters.title: The Family of Imran & Maryam's Dedication
chapters.summary: Maryam's mother dedicates her unborn child to Allah's service. Zakariya is appointed her guardian. He finds provisions with her from Allah.

chapters.title: Zakariya's Prayer for a Son
chapters.summary: Inspired by Maryam's provisions, the elderly Zakariya prays for a son and is granted Yahya (John).

chapters.title: The Annunciation
chapters.summary: The angel Jibril appears to Maryam and announces that she will bear a pure son — Isa, the Messiah.

chapters.title: The Miraculous Birth
chapters.summary: Maryam gives birth alone beneath a palm tree. She is told to shake it for fresh dates and to drink from the stream.

chapters.title: Isa Speaks from the Cradle
chapters.summary: Maryam brings the baby to her people. They accuse her, but the infant Isa speaks, declaring himself a servant of Allah.

chapters.title: Isa's Mission & Miracles
chapters.summary: Isa heals the blind, cures lepers, raises the dead by Allah's permission, and creates a bird from clay.

chapters.title: Isa's Nature & Ascension
chapters.summary: Allah clarifies that Isa was not killed or crucified — it was made to appear so. Allah raised him to Himself.

narratives.description: A righteous ruler who traveled to the ends of the earth and built a barrier against Gog and Magog

chapters.title: Journey to the West
chapters.summary: Dhul-Qarnayn travels to where the sun sets and finds a people. He punishes the wrongdoers and rewards the righteous.

chapters.title: Journey to the East
chapters.summary: He reaches the place of the rising sun and finds a people with no shelter from it.

chapters.title: The Barrier Against Gog & Magog
chapters.summary: A people suffering from Ya'juj and Ma'juj ask for help. Dhul-Qarnayn builds a massive barrier of iron and copper, declaring it is a mercy from Allah.

narratives.description: Young believers who fled persecution and slept in a cave for 309 years by Allah's decree

chapters.title: The Flight to the Cave
chapters.summary: Young men who believed in Allah flee from their idolatrous society and take refuge in a cave, trusting in Allah's mercy.

chapters.title: The Deep Sleep
chapters.summary: Allah causes them to sleep for 309 years. The sun passes their cave without disturbing them. Their dog stretches at the entrance.

chapters.title: The Awakening
chapters.summary: They awaken thinking they slept only a day. One goes to the city for food and discovers centuries have passed. People dispute their number.

chapters.title: The Lesson
chapters.summary: Allah reveals they remained 309 years. The story teaches that Allah's promise is true and the Hour is coming.

narratives.description: Timeless parental wisdom from Luqman the Wise — a guide to faith, character, and conduct

chapters.title: The Foundation: Tawheed
chapters.summary: Luqman advises his son: Do not associate anything with Allah. Indeed, associating others with Him is a great injustice.

chapters.title: Honor Your Parents
chapters.summary: Be grateful to Allah and to your parents. But if they urge you to associate with Allah what you have no knowledge of, do not obey them — yet still keep their company in this world with kindness.

chapters.title: Allah Sees Everything
chapters.summary: Even something as small as a mustard seed, hidden in a rock or in the heavens or earth — Allah will bring it forth.

chapters.title: Prayer, Patience, and Character
chapters.summary: Establish prayer, enjoin good, forbid evil, be patient. Do not turn your cheek in contempt or walk arrogantly — Allah does not love the boastful. Be moderate in your pace and lower your voice.

narratives.description: The ultimate test of patience — Ayyub loses everything yet never loses faith

chapters.title: The Trial
chapters.summary: Ayyub is afflicted with severe illness and hardship. He calls out to Allah: 'Adversity has touched me, and You are the Most Merciful of the merciful.'

chapters.title: The Healing
chapters.summary: Allah commands him to strike the ground with his foot — a cool spring gushes forth. His health and family are restored, doubled as a mercy.

narratives.description: The prophet who left his people in frustration, was swallowed by a whale, and repented in its belly

chapters.title: Departure & the Whale
chapters.summary: Yunus leaves his people in anger. He boards a ship, is cast overboard, and is swallowed by a great fish. In darkness, he calls out: 'There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers.'

chapters.title: Salvation & His People's Repentance
chapters.summary: Allah saves Yunus and casts him ashore, weak. Meanwhile, his people repent and are the only nation in the Quran saved from punishment after initially rejecting their prophet.

narratives.description: The prophet sent to Madyan who called his people to honest trade and fair dealing

chapters.title: The Call to Justice in Trade
chapters.summary: Shu'ayb warns the people of Madyan against cheating in weights and measures, and corruption in the land.

chapters.title: Rejection & Destruction
chapters.summary: The people mock Shu'ayb and threaten to expel him. An earthquake seizes them and they are found lifeless in their homes.

narratives.description: Lut's mission to a people committing unprecedented sin, and their ultimate destruction

chapters.title: Warning His People
chapters.summary: Lut calls his people to abandon their transgression. They mock him and threaten to expel him.

chapters.title: The Angels' Visit
chapters.summary: Angels visit Lut in human form. The people descend upon his house. The angels reveal their mission and tell Lut to leave by night.

chapters.title: The Destruction
chapters.summary: At dawn, the cities are turned upside down and pelted with stones of baked clay. Lut's wife is among those who perish.
